channel.title: Journal of Ancient History
channel.description: A RSS feed containing the latest articles published in Journal of Ancient History

item.title: Social Biographies of the Ancient World. Studying Ahatabu, Jonathan, and Babatha through a Bourdieusian Approach: Towards a New Historiographical Habitus
item.description: Article by Helen Dixon DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/jah-2024-0017

item.title: Ancient Near Eastern Field Theory: Adapting Bourdieu for Social Biographies of the Ancient World
item.description: Article by Helen Dixon DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/jah-2024-0022

item.title: Ahatabu and her Stela (ÄM 7707): Funerary Habitus in Achaemenid Egypt
item.description: Article by Helen Dixon DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/jah-2024-0018

item.title: Functional Differentiation in 1 Maccabees: Exploring Second Century BCE Judean Society Through the Character of Jonathan Apphus
item.description: Article by Helen Dixon DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/jah-2024-0023

item.title: Falling Out with the In-Laws. Understanding the Babatha Archive with Pierre Bourdieu’s Field Theory and Theory of Practice.
item.description: Article by Helen Dixon DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/jah-2024-0025

item.title: Ancient Individuals and Bourdieu in Context: A Historical Anthropological Response
item.description: Article by Helen Dixon DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/jah-2024-0019

item.title: A Sociological Response: Challenging the Modernity-centrism of Pierre Bourdieu’s Field Approach
item.description: Article by Helen Dixon DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/jah-2024-0020

item.title: A Levantine Archaeological Response: Thinking with Bourdieu though Limited Data and Explicit Assumptions
item.description: Article by Helen Dixon DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/jah-2024-0021
